title: Duality Wilting
definition: The realization and discarding of the subject-object duality in favor of direct, unfiltered experience.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The idea that one can experience reality directly, without the need for a separate observer or subject-object duality.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A philosophical framework that advocates for direct experience and the rejection of the traditional subject-object separation.
difficulty_levels.expert: Through deep observation, the notion of a separate observer learning about a world is seen as illusory, leading to the dissolution of the subject-object duality.

extra_fields.insight: Through deep observation, the notion of a separate observer learning about a world is seen as illusory, leading to the dissolution of the subject-object duality.
extra_fields.historical_context: Rooted in various Eastern philosophies and mysticism, it reflects a growing interest in direct experience and the deconstruction of traditional Western dualistic thinking.
limitations: Criticized for potential lack of scientific rigor, difficulty in practical application, and challenges to traditional western philosophical paradigms.
applications: Mindfulness practices, certain forms of meditation, and existential exploration.
